tex: Write the number one hundred and three thousand eight hundred and six in figures.

Simplify.

$3x - 4x + 7x$
Work out the area of a rectangle that is 9.5 m long and 6.8 m wide.

The probability of picking a red sweet from a bag is 0.05.
Find the probability of not picking a red sweet.
Work out the value of $\dfrac{mk^3}{3}$ when $m = 4$ and $k = 7$.

A box, in the shape of a cuboid, has volume 357 cm$^3$.
It has a length of 8.5 cm and a width of 6 cm.
Calculate the height of the box.

These are the masses, in kg, of 12 parcels.
0.3 \quad 0.4 \quad 1.2 \quad 0.8 \quad 1.1 \quad 2.1 \quad 1.7 \quad 1.8 \quad 1.2 \quad 2.3 \quad 0.7 \quad 1.1
(a) Complete the stem-and-leaf diagram for the 12 parcels.

\begin{tabular}{c|l}
0 & 3 \quad 4 \\
1 & \\
2 & \\
\end{tabular}
 Key: $0|3$ represents 0.3 kg

(b) Find the median.

The grid shows point $P$ and point $R$.

(a) Write down the coordinates of point $P$.

(b) $\vec{PQ} = \begin{pmatrix} 3 \\ -2 \end{pmatrix}$\\
Mark point $Q$ on the grid.

(c) Find $\vec{QR}$.

Simplify.

(a) $\dfrac{y^3}{y^5}$

(b) $7x^0$

The scatter diagram shows the number of visitors and the total amount spent, in thousands of dollars, at a zoo on each of eight days.

%\begin{InsertScreenShot}
%    
%\end{InsertScreenShot}

(a) On one of the eight days there are 410 visitors.
Find the total amount spent by visitors during this day.

\$ 

(b) Information for the ninth day is shown in the table.
\begin{tabular}{|l|c|}
\hline
Number of visitors & 175 \\
\hline
Total amount spent (\$1000) & 9 \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
Plot this information on the scatter diagram.

(c) Draw a line of best fit on the scatter diagram.

(d) On the tenth day the total amount spent is \$22\,000.
Estimate the number of visitors on this day.
texHtml:
The scatter diagram shows the number of visitors and the total amount spent, in thousands of dollars, at a zoo on each of eight days. %% % (a) On one of the eight days there are 410 visitors. Find the total amount spent by visitors during this day. \$ (b) Information for the ninth day is shown in the table.
Number of visitors175
Total amount spent (\$1000)9
Plot this information on the scatter diagram. (c) Draw a line of best fit on the scatter diagram. (d) On the tenth day the total amount spent is \$22\,000. Estimate the number of visitors on this day.

The diagram shows two right-angled triangles, $ABC$ and $PQR$.

(a) Complete this statement with a geometrical term.

Triangle $ABC$ is  to triangle $PQR$.

(b) Calculate angle $ABC$.
Find the lowest common multiple (LCM) of 32 and 40.

Joe thinks of a number, $n$, trebles it, and subtracts 5.
The result is 22.
Write this as an equation in terms of $n$, and solve the equation.

Dominic asks 30 students in his class if they are right-handed or left-handed.
7 students are left-handed.
Work out the expected number of left-handed students in the whole school of 960 students.

Without using a calculator, work out $4\frac{1}{6} - 1\frac{7}{8}$.
You must show all your working and give your answer as a mixed number in its simplest form.
